name: Valeriana officinalis
summary: Valerian may support sleep onset and nighttime relaxation, with results varying by root preparation.
description: Sleep-onset and relaxation herb; preparation quality matters.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: Valerian Root in Treating Sleep Problems and Associated Disorders-A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

sources.title: v9.5: Systematic review suggests possible sleep-quality benefit but trials were heterogeneous and often methodologically weak; keep below melatonin/magnesium.

claimMap.claim: v9.5: Systematic review suggests possible sleep-quality benefit but trials were heterogeneous and often methodologically weak; keep below melatonin/magnesium.
claimMap.notes: v9.5: Systematic review suggests possible sleep-quality benefit but trials were heterogeneous and often methodologically weak; keep below melatonin/magnesium.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Valerian extract doses vary; many sleep trials used 300–600 mg before bed

dosage: Standardized extract (0.3-0.8% valerenic acids): 400-600 mg taken 30-60 min before sleep; tea preparations less standardized
typical_dosage: Standardized extract (0.3-0.8% valerenic acids): 400-600 mg taken 30-60 min before sleep; tea preparations less standardized
